title: How to save that dance challenge video before it disappears
tldr: You found a killer dance challenge on TikTok but don't want to lose it when the trend fades. Here's how to grab it as MP4 or MP3 for free using Saveik.
description: Learn how to download TikTok dance challenge videos as MP4 or MP3 for free with Saveik. Works on phone and computer. No sign-up needed.
author: Saveik Team
content:
My cousin sent me a dance challenge video last night. It was one of those Renegade-style routines with a beat that gets stuck in your head. I wanted to save it so I could practice later — but TikTok's download button was missing. Classic.

So I did what I always do. I grabbed the link, opened Saveik, and had the MP4 in about 10 seconds. No app. No sign-up. Just the video.

## Why you'd want to save a dance challenge

Dance challenges are weirdly temporary. One week everyone's doing the "Savage Love" steps. Next week it's forgotten. But some of these routines are genuinely fun. You might want to:

- Learn the moves at your own pace (pause, slow down, rewatch)
- Share with friends who don't have TikTok
- Keep it for a workout playlist
- Extract the audio for a ringtone or edit

I've saved at least a dozen dance challenge videos this year. Some I still watch when I need a mood boost.

## How to download a dance challenge video (MP4 or MP3)

It's stupid simple. Here's the exact steps I use:

1. Open TikTok and find the dance challenge video you want.
2. Tap the "Share" icon (the arrow) and select "Copy Link".
3. Go to Saveik.com in your browser — phone or computer, doesn't matter.
4. Paste the link in the box and hit download.
5. Choose MP4 (video) or MP3 (audio only). Wait a few seconds.

The file saves right to your device. On iPhone it goes to your Files app. On Android it's in Downloads. On computer it's wherever your browser puts stuff.

**Pro tip:** If you only want the song from the dance challenge, pick MP3. I've done this for a few tracks that aren't on Spotify. The audio quality is surprisingly good — clear enough for my car speakers.

## Two real scenarios

**Scenario 1: Learning the routine**

My friend Sarah wanted to learn a K-pop dance challenge for a party. She had no TikTok account (her choice). I downloaded the video for her as MP4, sent it via text, and she practiced for two days. By the party, she had the moves down. No account needed.

**Scenario 2: Building a workout playlist**

I'm not a dancer, but I run. And some dance challenge songs have perfect BPMs for jogging. I downloaded a few as MP3s, threw them in a playlist, and now I have a "running beats" mix that actually motivates me. Way better than generic workout music.

## Why not just use TikTok's download?

TikTok lets you download some videos, but not all. Creators can turn it off. And even when it works, the download has a watermark. Saveik gives you the clean version — no TikTok logo, no username overlay. Just the video or audio.

Also, TikTok's downloads are MP4 only. If you want MP3, you need a third-party tool anyway.

## It works on everything

I've used Saveik on my iPhone, my Windows laptop, and my friend's Android tablet. Same steps. Same speed. No app installation. The site is mobile-friendly, so the buttons are big enough to tap without zooming.

One thing I like: there's no limit. Download as many dance challenge videos as you want. I've grabbed like 20 in one sitting before.

## Quick tip: saving for offline use

If you're going somewhere with spotty signal (subway, camping, whatever), download the videos beforehand. I do this before flights. Pop them in a folder, watch later without data. The MP4s are usually around 5-10 MB each, so they don't eat up space.

## Final thought

Dance challenges are meant to be shared. If you find one that makes you smile, save it. You never know when you'll want to revisit it — or learn the steps for a party.

If this helped you grab that dance challenge video, cool. If you have questions, drop them below. I check comments.

Happy dancing.

faq.q: Does Saveik work for dance challenge videos that are private?
faq.a: Nope. You can only download public videos. If the account is private or the video is deleted, it won't work. But for any public dance challenge, it's fine.

faq.q: Can I download just the audio from a dance challenge?
faq.a: Yes. When you paste the link, you get the option to download as MP3 instead of MP4. I use this for songs that aren't on streaming services.

faq.q: Will I get in trouble for downloading dance challenge videos?
faq.a: Downloading for personal use is generally fine. Don't re-upload and claim it as your own. That's rude. But saving for yourself? No big deal.
